title: Decoder producer/ranker service-time compatibility
hypothesis: The lowest-power serial r64/k1 ranker points may be preferable to fully parallel rank trees if their tile service time fits within the output-projection producer cadence.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Which measured r64/k1 ranker point remains off the throughput path when coupled to the output-projection producer service model?
include: measured serial lanes_per_cycle 1, 2, 4, 8, and 16 ranker points
measured fully parallel radix-2, radix-4, and radix-8 rank-tree points
existing stage-serialized output-projection producer service rows
single-r64 ranker and banked-r64 ranker assumptions for wider producer tiles

expected_benefit: turns the standalone serial-ranker measurements into a producer-coupled choice
identifies when serial rankers are throughput-safe versus when rank trees are needed
keeps the next producer-coupled RTL target grounded in measured PPA and service cycles
risks: producer cadence is still analytical and should later be replaced by measured producer output statistics
banked-r64 integration scales measured ranker PPA linearly and does not model shared placement effects
ready-valid correctness still relies on the prior equivalence and standalone ranker simulations

required_evaluations.objective: Compare measured serial and rank-tree r64/k1 ranker service cycles against output-projection producer cadence.

expected_result.reason: If serial lpc1/lpc2 remains throughput-safe for the selected producer rows, use it as the next producer-coupled RTL candidate; otherwise use the lowest-cost rank tree that avoids producer backpressure.
